description: Subclass method signature is incompatible with the base class method signature.
reasoning: The base class _LinearPlotter defines the plot method with a single parameter 'ax' (line 66). The subclass _RegressionPlotter overrides this method but changes the signature to require three mandatory parameters: 'ax', 'scatter_kws', and 'line_kws' (line 351). This violates LSP because a caller holding a reference to the base type _LinearPlotter cannot substitute it with a _RegressionPlotter without the program failing due to a TypeError when calling plot(ax).

description: Subclass overrides configuration attributes in a way that breaks the base class contract for data ingestion.
reasoning: VectorPlotter defines a contract for wide-form data via wide_structure where 'x' maps to the index ('@index'). _CategoricalPlotter overrides this (line 51) to map 'x' to columns ('@columns'), fundamentally changing how data is interpreted. It also removes 'x' from flat_structure (line 52). Furthermore, the __init__ method (lines 75-89) performs a post-initialization mutation by swapping 'x' and 'y' variables in self.plot_data and self.variables if orientation is horizontal, which invalidates the state established by the base class constructor and breaks the expectation that variable assignments are consistent across the VectorPlotter hierarchy.

description: Subclass overrides class attributes defining data structures, violating the base class contract for data ingestion.
reasoning: Similar to _CategoricalPlotter, _DistributionPlotter overrides wide_structure (line 101) and flat_structure (line 102). In wide_structure, it maps 'x' to '@values' instead of the base class's '@index'. In flat_structure, it removes the 'y' variable entirely. These changes alter the fundamental behavior of the assign_variables method inherited from VectorPlotter, meaning code expecting the standard data ingestion behavior of a VectorPlotter will receive incorrect or incomplete data when using this subclass.
